reference: Hello. Yes, I would say palpitations accompanied by light-headedness likely indicate some serious type of arrhythmia. You are fortunate that in thirty years, you have not passed out, but there is a risk that this type of arrhythmia can be fatal. I would suggest visiting a cardiologist. A repeat electrocardiogram and echocardiogram may be necessary, and you might need Holter monitoring for twenty-four hours. Ultimately, you might require medication or a pacemaker depending on the severity and type of arrhythmias. I suspect it to be supraventricular tachycardia or ventricular tachycardia runs.
text: Hello. Yes, I would say palpitations accompanied by lightheadedness likely indicate some serious type of arrhythmia. You are fortunate that in 30 years you have not passed out, but there is a risk that this type of arrhythmia can be fatal. I would suggest visiting a cardiologist. A repeat electrocardiogram and echocardiogram may be necessary, and you might need Holter monitoring for 24 hours. Ultimately, you might require medication or a pacemaker, depending on the severity and type of arrhythmias. I suspect it to be supraventricular tachycardia or ventricular tachycardia runs.
